title: quercus xylina
intro: Quercus xylina, also known as the 'Yellow Buckeye' or 'Black Buckeye', is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus xylina include supporting immune function, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Quercus xylina can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of illnesses.
